change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API

en.value:
1. Assess macroeconomic impacts (including external balances, fiscal and monetary effects) 
• Obtain projections of revenue share, royalty revenue, tax revenue, and equity dividends from the project’s financial model, and compare these inflows to outflows (e.g., debt repayment schedule) to determine Government’s net cash position.
• Obtain macroeconomic information from Government. Conduct consultations as necessary.
• Conduct analysis and document/present findings.
 
2. Conduct impact assessment on Governments’ financial plans and budget
• Obtain Government budget projection data. Conduct consultations as necessary.
• Take information from 1 above, conduct analysis and document the findings.
 
3. Conduct public finance and budget analysis to design governance measures and monitoring mechanisms for the handling of revenues and expenditures from the WSHP
• Hold workshops with relevant Government agencies to determine current practices.
• Make recommendations/design as appropriate.
 
4. Analyze the potential for economic growth and social development through project benefits
• Assess the sub regional costs and benefits.
• Conduct surveys and hold stakeholder workshops as necessary.
 
5. Perform project-related financial, economic, social, poverty reduction and institutional analyses
 
6. Prepare a small-scale area development program for project-affected areas
• Conduct stakeholder workshops and surveys as necessary.

snapshot.executing_agency: Ministry of Energy, Water Resources and Irrigation
snapshot.implementing_agency: Ministry of Energy, Water Resources and Irrigation

en.full: TA 4985-Nepal for Preparing the West Seti Hydroelectric Project

version_summary.change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API
